assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the palace and gardens of Versailles?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3928cd0685378d6f17016a8caf4463cf.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Louis XIV filled both the palace and its gardens with sculpture, painting, and fountains that all focused on himself.**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1600-1700-the-baroque/france/a/chteau-de-versailles)**

choices.content: Louis XIV built the palace and gardens of Versailles when he decided to move his court out of Paris.
Versailles stands as a prime example of the over-the-top excesses of the French nobility that led to the French Revolution.
The outside of the Versailles was influenced by classical architecture.
The decoration of Versailles revolves around Christian themes of charity and humility.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Louis XIV was not content with the restraint of pure classical design. He had his team create a palace that used classical structures to contain the elaborate grandeur of the Baroque style.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3928cd0685378d6f17016a8caf4463cf.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The exterior of the building is classical and houses a grand Baroque interior.**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1600-1700-the-baroque/france/a/chteau-de-versailles)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following artists and architects was NOT involved in the building or decoration of Versailles?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3928cd0685378d6f17016a8caf4463cf.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The following individuals are responsible for the architecture and decoration of the palace and gardens of Versailles: Louis le Vau (architect to the aristocracy), André le Nôtre (landscape designer extraordinaire), and Charles le Brun (über-fashionable interior decorator and painter).**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1600-1700-the-baroque/france/a/chteau-de-versailles)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Louis XIV called himself the Sun King and linked himself to the Greek sun god [[☃ dropdown 1]].**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3928cd0685378d6f17016a8caf4463cf.png)
hints:
**Louis XIV called himself the Sun King and linked himself to the Greek sun god Apollo.**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1600-1700-the-baroque/france/a/chteau-de-versailles)**
